scan_verification.readout_roles: ramsey.xml full_experiment=0: readout 1 mS=0 reference; readout 2 Ramsey signal after Ramsey pulses

expected_signal_model.source: job.metadata.expected_signal_model plus direct recomputation of target bins

model_from_job.det_shift_test_rationale: The terminal weak-pi center is only about +40 kHz from the prior strong-pi center, so the previous ~1.9 MHz Ramsey component is not explained by a +0.4 MHz electron-resonance mis-centering. Changing the programmed Ramsey detuning from 1.5 MHz to 1.0 MHz moves true 13C sidebands to about det +/- 0.3846 MHz (0.615/1.385 MHz), separating them from the prior 1.9 MHz ambiguity and testing whether spectral power follows the programmed carrier/sidebands.
model_from_job.distinguishability: At 800k shots per point, the binomial floor is about 0.112%; a conservative 5% Ramsey oscillation is ~44.7x that floor, and a 1% sideband-scale feature is ~8.9x before drift/systematics. The 10 us span gives 100 kHz FFT bins, separating the carrier and 13C sidebands by about 3.85 bins.
model_from_job.drift_basis: Terminal 8-average Ramsey and terminal 4-average weak-pi pODMR both had scan-order-aware snake drift diagnostics with no flagged averages; the planned 16 even averages preserve forward/reverse balance, with 50k reps per average to keep the per-average tracking window inside the nighttime advisory cap if MATLAB advisory agrees.

interpretation.summary: Terminal 16-average det=1.0 MHz Ramsey completed with healthy counts and no stop request. Readout2/self-baseline FFT target amplitudes are direct 13C 0.14%, det-13C 0.73%, carrier 0.41%, det+13C 0.84%, and previous 1.9 MHz ambiguity 0.56%. The strongest target bin is det_plus_13C_hz; the largest self-baseline FFT peak is 0.700 MHz at 0.89%. The det+13C-compatible component is a real candidate because it moved from the prior det=1.5 MHz high-sideband region to the new det=1.0 MHz high-sideband region, but these sub-percent-to-about-percent features are much smaller than the pODMR contrast and the low sideband/direct-Larmor/carrier support is incomplete. Damped-cosine fits give a short-us order T2* scale but are sensitive to frequency/window/early-tau handling, so report only a bounded short-us order rather than a final scalar.
interpretation.recommended_next: Do not claim 13C from this run alone. Because a weak det+13C-compatible component is present but incomplete, perform a bridge-free follow-up-condition synthesis; a third det-shift Ramsey (for example det=1.25 MHz) is the targeted next experiment only if the model/advisory/verifier gates pass and the bridge remains idle.
